verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 directly answers how it works: "giving the client a signed, self-contained token after login"; B first defines the object before the auth flow.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleanly to the tradeoff and recommendation: "Reach for JWTs when... multiple services" and "single-server web app... session... simpler and safer."
verdict.fabrication: B adds an unsupported precise figure: "5–15 min"; A makes no similarly precise unsupported numeric claim.
verdict.actionability: B is slightly easier to implement from because it names concrete checks like "validate exp, iss, aud" and cookie protections "HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite."
